welcome.title: Welcome to PFinTrack!
welcome.description: Start tracking your finances from scratch, or explore the app features with realistic sample data first.

tour.skipDescription: You haven't opened the {module} tab yet. Skip this section and continue to the next module?

steps.tx1: All your financial records are here — income, expenses, and transfers between wallets. Tap this tab to get started.
steps.tx2: Swipe left/right or tap the arrows to navigate between dates. Tap the date to open the calendar.
steps.tx3: Today's total income, expenses, and balance are shown here.
steps.tx4: Tap to add a new transaction — choose Income, Expense, or Transfer.
steps.tx5: Tap to change the sort order — by date or transaction amount.
steps.tx6: Tap to export all transactions to an Excel file.
steps.tx7: Tap to view the full transaction history across all dates.
steps.wl1: Wallet is your account or cash wallet. All transactions are linked to a wallet. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.wl2: This card shows the total balance across all your wallets.
steps.wl3: Filter wallets by type — bank, e-wallet, savings, and more.
steps.ln1: Record your debts and receivables here — who owes you, or who you owe. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.ln2: 'Get': you borrow from someone or someone repays their debt to you. 'Give': you lend money to someone or you pay off your debt.
steps.ln3: Every person or entity is grouped here. Tap to view their loan history.
steps.rp1: Your financial report is here — income, expenses, and balance summary. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.rp2: View reports by period: today realtime, monthly, or custom date range.
steps.rp3: Switch between Expense and Income view to see the breakdown chart for each.
steps.end: Done! You now know the main features of PFinTrack. Start recording your finances now.
steps.wl5: Tap to add a new wallet — bank account, cash, GoPay, and more.
steps.wl6: Tap a wallet card to view details, edit balance, or see its transaction history.
steps.wl4: Sort wallets by name or balance to find them quickly.
steps.rp4: This chart shows the distribution of your expenses or income by category. Tap a slice to filter the list below.
steps.rp5: Tap to create a custom report for a specific date range.

changelog.title: What's New
changelog.subtitle: The story behind every PFinTrack update

entries.intro: Small touches that make every interaction feel more natural.
items: Money moves between wallets in perfect sync — your balances always line up
Indonesian screen readers now feel right at home with fully localized accessibility labels
Icon badges and quick menus look unified across the app
The app launches faster and stays rock-steady from the very first second

entries.intro: Final touches that make the experience even smoother.
items: The onboarding tour now looks clean and focused
Confirmation dialogs feel more natural and accessibility-friendly
Switching tabs in Reports is instant
Long lists in Wallet & Loan scroll more smoothly

entries.intro: The very start of PFinTrack — manage your personal finance from one place.
items: Track wallets, edit balances manually, and follow every change
Log income, expenses, and transfers between wallets
Manage loans with automatic settlement detection
Real-time, monthly, and custom reports tailored to you
Backup, theme, language, and even demo data — all in one place
Works offline and installs like a native app

items.a: All your financial data — wallets, transactions, loans — is stored locally on your device, inside your browser's storage. PFinTrack has no server and never sends your data anywhere. It's yours, on your device, full stop.

items.q: What happens if I switch phones or lose my device?
items.a: Since your data lives on your device, switching phones without a backup means losing it. The fix is simple: go to Settings → Data & Storage → Export before you switch. Save the backup file somewhere safe — Google Drive, your gallery, anywhere. Then import it on your new device.

items.q: Can PFinTrack see my financial data?
items.a: No. PFinTrack has no accounts, no login, and no server connection. Your data is 100% on your device and can't be accessed by anyone else — including the PFinTrack team. We don't even know you exist.

items.q: How do I back up and restore my data?
items.a: Go to Settings → Data & Storage. From there you can Export your data as a .json or .gz file, and Import to restore it. It's a good habit to back up regularly, especially before switching phones or doing a major browser update.

items.q: How do I delete all my data?
items.a: Go to Settings → Data & Storage and choose the option to delete all data. This permanently removes all your wallets, transactions, and loan records. It can't be undone, so make sure you've exported a backup first if you might need it later.

items.q: What's the difference between Give and Receive in the Loan module?
items.a: Give means you lent money to someone — they owe you. Receive means you received money from them — either they're paying you back, or you're the one paying off a debt. Everything is grouped by person so you can track it in one place.

items.q: Why doesn't a Transfer count as an expense?
items.a: A transfer just moves money between your own wallets — your total net worth stays the same. It's different from an expense, where money actually leaves your pocket. Counting it as an expense would make your reports look worse than they actually are.

items.q: What is "Balance Correction" in my transaction list?
items.a: This is an automatic entry that appears when you manually edit a wallet's balance. For example, if you change your BCA balance from Rp 500,000 to Rp 750,000, PFinTrack records the Rp 250,000 difference as a Balance Correction so the change is always traceable.

items.q: What is Saving Rate and how is it calculated?
items.a: Saving Rate shows what percentage of your income you actually kept during a period. The formula is: (Income − Expenses) ÷ Income × 100%. If your income is Rp 10 million and expenses Rp 7 million, your Saving Rate is 30%. Higher is better.

items.q: Why can my wallet balance go negative?
items.a: This can happen when you record an expense or loan that exceeds a wallet's available balance. PFinTrack doesn't block it because that's how real life works — credit cards and overdraft accounts can genuinely go negative. A negative balance isn't a glitch, it's valid data.

items.q: Can I use PFinTrack without an internet connection?
items.a: Yes, completely. PFinTrack is a PWA (Progressive Web App) that can be installed and used 100% offline. All features — logging transactions, viewing reports, managing wallets — work without internet. You only need a connection the very first time you open the app.

Demo Mode fills the app with sample data — fake wallets, transactions, and loans — so you can explore all features without entering real data first. Turn it on from Settings, and clear it anytime using the red button in Settings when you're ready to start fresh with your own data.

items.q: How do I restart the guided tour?
items.a: Go to Settings → scroll to the Help section → tap "View Tutorial". The tour will restart from the beginning and walk you through all the main features again. Handy if you just reset your data or want a quick refresher.
Yes. PFinTrack is completely free — no premium tier, no subscription, no ads, no in-app purchases. Every feature available now and in future Fase 1 updates is free for everyone.
